pekan kemaren gua kasih gratis agent gua ke hampir 100 orang buat test. agent yang sama yang hasilin gua 20$ per hari atau lebih.

gua open lagi buat 50 orang di komen.

$189.7 dalam 11 hari aktif. satu merah di tanggal 18, sisanya hijau semua. minggu ke-4 yang paling kenceng, $101.95 dalam satu hari di tanggal 22.

banyak yang langsung ikut build, nambahin fitur sendiri di atas framework yang gua kasih. ada yang nambah memory, ada yang bikin dashboard, ada yang upgrade screening pipeline. ada juga yang lurking doang, dan itu fine.

dalam hampir 2 minggu, agent gua kebanyakan lakuin 3 setup:

bid-ask tight di new pair yang volume-nya lagi tinggi. masuk cepet, keluar cepet, ambil fee dari volatilitas awal.

bid-ask 45-50 bins di token yang udah koreksi. ini yang paling sering. token udah turun, volume masih ada, range pas buat nangkap rebound sambil collect fees.

bid-ask default di token yang volume-nya masih jalan tapi belum koreksi banyak. lebih konservatif, hold lebih lama, tapi tetap profitable.

agent ini jalan 24 jam nonstop. gua cuma define cara entry dan exit biar range-nya efisien, sisanya dia handle sendiri.

sejauh ini masih profitable dan masih evolve.

rosie-sol again. 1 day 21 hours, $63, same strat as always this month.  the best play in this current market is multi-hours or multi-days on recent runners that are slowing down and entering price discovery.

not the token that pumped 10x yesterday. the one that pumped, cooled off, and is now finding its floor.  

this strat has been building my gains brick by brick in march. not flashy. not 5 quick flips a day. just solid positions on the right tokens, fees accumulating, exit on the pump, repeat.  

the tradeoff is you can't spam in and out with new pairs like you would with quick plays. but your mental is way more stable. 

you're not glued to the chart every minutes, you're not panic closing on every red candle.  one thing to note: 

this strat needs more capital to really work well. ideally you're running 3 positions at the same time on different tokens.

one position multi-day with mid capital isn't enough to feel the compounding, but 3 running simultaneously starts to build stable daily gain,  that said, if you're still growing your port, quick in and out is still the better thing to learn first.

you build your read on market timing, token selection, and exit discipline way faster when you're doing 10 positions a day than when you're holding overnight.  @MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

reason: Perps / Trading cluster linked by [volume, chart, short, long, solana]

sampleTweets.text:
together-sol, 42 seconds, $52, 10.66%. not my usual play this month but when volume hits 500k per 5 minutes you don't just sit there.

bonk narrative was strong at the start. that's already a green flag before i even look at the chart. 

i played this strat 3 times, clean 10% each exit, before the TVL started exploding and the pool got too crowded to make it worth it.

setup was spot tight, 30 bins, single side sol only. tight because the volume was violent enough to fill bins fast and i wanted to be in and out before the momentum faded.

the timing window on this kind of play is short. once TVL balloons up the fee per bin drops and your edge disappears. i got in early, caught the volume, and stopped touching it when the pool got thick.

multi-days is still my main strat this month. but this is a good reminder that sometimes the market just hands you a setup that's too clean to ignore.

🚨BREAKING: Anza has introduced Constellation, a multiple concurrent proposers protocol for @Solana with 50ms cycles, claiming the fastest protocol enforced economic tick rate of any blockchain.

It replaces the single leader model with concurrent proposers, removing exclusive control over transaction inclusion and ordering. Fairness is enforced at the protocol level without relying on trusted third parties or modified clients, while validator economics remain largely unchanged.

.@useSolami is building the complete Solana infra stack - staked RPCs, gRPC streams, ShredStream and SWQoS

Staked RPCs route through validators that don’t fold under pressure. gRPC streams deliver real-time data without polling. ShredStream puts you ahead of the chain with raw fragments before full block assembly.

SWQoS skips gossip and lands txs direct to the slot leader. Parsed transactions decode raw Solana output instantly. REST APIs cover balances, history, and metadata out of the box.

Free to start with a 2-day free trial. No credit card. Just build.

The focus is clear: every layer of execution and data, for builders and traders who refuse to slow down.

removing scheduler competition like this simply results in any critical mass mev client conspiring with any single proposer to publish whatever they want whilst weaponizing their network to censor whatever they don't with 100% certainty.

reason: Ecosystem Updates cluster linked by [update, chart, meteora]

sampleTweets.text:
new feature on @MeteoraAG just dropped price charting on main app and this is genuinely one of the most useful UI updates for multi-days LPers.

before this, setting a range meant opening a separate chart on another tab, eyeballing where support sits, estimating your range width, then jumping back to Meteora and hoping you got it close enough. 

now you just drag directly on the chart and see exactly where your position will land before you deploy.

for multi-days this is a big deal. the whole strategy depends on entering after a meaningful dip and placing your range in the right zone. 

being able to see support visually on the same screen where you're setting the range makes that decision faster and more precise.

the other thing that's genuinely useful: your open positions now show up as lines directly on the chart.

i used to add horizontal lines manually on gmgn just to see where my range sat relative to price. that's gone now. 

you can see your active range on the chart in real time, which makes monitoring a multi-days or quick play position a lot cleaner.

you can actually see the price move in your range!

small update on paper but it removes two things that used to slow down every single entry decision.
